h0: Mean math scores are equal across all 5 race_ethnicity groups.
h1: At least one race_ethnicity group has a significantly different mean math score.
justification: Largest math_score spread across multi-group predictors (12.19 pts). ANOVA is appropriate for k>2 independent groups with a continuous outcome. Levene test confirmed homogeneity of variance.
secondary_note: Secondary option: Pearson correlation (reading_score × writing_score, r=0.9546). Strong but less analytically rich than ANOVA (no group differences, no post-hoc).

h0: The rank distribution of math scores is identical across all 5 race_ethnicity groups.
h1: At least one race_ethnicity group has a systematically different rank distribution of math scores.
justification: Direct non-parametric mirror of ANOVA. Some subgroups fail normality; Kruskal-Wallis requires no normality assumption. Pairing with ANOVA enables direct comparison of parametric vs rank-based results on the same variables.
secondary_note: Secondary option: Spearman correlation (paired with Pearson above). Equally valid but offers less group-level insight.
